steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says almost all Joyce scholars with expertise in textual issues worked on the Gabler edition, leaving very few independent experts to review it. Reviewers were therefore likely outsiders: either Joyce scholars without deep editorial knowledge, or editorial experts without deep Joyce knowledge. The blank must describe the tone of those initial reviews and the logical reason behind it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'negative, since those Joyce scholars with the necessary expertise...': the passage says most experts worked with Gabler and would not review their own edition — so expert critics were largely absent, not writing negative reviews. ✗
  • Choice B — 'positive, since scholars who reviewed the 1984 edition were unaffiliated with its production and were mostly either Joyce specialists who were largely unfamiliar with editorial theories and practices or specialists in such theories and practices who were insufficiently familiar with Joyce.': reviewers lacked the combined expertise to spot Gabler’s errors, so positive initial reviews are exactly what the passage predicts. ✓
  • Choice C — 'negative, since any scholar with expertise in both editorial traditions most likely worked with Gabler...': same flaw as A — the lack of outside experts predicts favorable, not negative, reviews. ✗
  • Choice D — 'positive since Ulysses is a novel in English and the 1984 edition would therefore be more widely reviewed in United States and United Kingdom publications...': geography of publication is never mentioned and is irrelevant to the passage’s logic. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
